reasoning: While the composite score is high, the shingling recall (0.636) is well below the 0.75 threshold, indicating significant content loss. This is corroborated by the fidelity report evidence describing a 50% loss of descriptive text on pages 2 and 3, which the signal incorrectly labeled as minor despite it meeting the criteria for a major defect.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The output fails to include the descriptive body text for several subsections. Specifically, the entire descriptive content (multiple paragraphs) for Section 6, items a, b, c, d, and e is missing. Additionally, the body text for Section 8, items a and b is missing. These omissions represent approximately 50% of the total text content on pages 2 and 3 of the source document, far exceeding the 25% threshold for a MAJOR defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The LGC last met on November 20th. All open slots on the committee have been filled... [approx 2 pages of text following subheadings in Section 6 and Section 8]
concerns.output_quote: [Content entirely absent after list items in reading_order 24, 26, and 30]
concerns.location: Section 6 (Status Reports) and Section 8 (Other Items), Pages 2 and 3

concerns.analysis: The document's list structures are poorly handled. The primary numbered list (1-5) on Page 1 is fragmented into five separate single-item unordered lists (elements 9, 11, 13, 15, 19), and the numbering prefixes are stripped. Furthermore, the alphabetical sub-list in section 6 (a-c) on Page 2 loses its labels ('a.', 'b.', 'c.') and is converted to an unordered list (element 24). In contrast, sub-lists later in the document (elements 26 and 30) retain their labels as part of the text string while
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1. Welcome
2. Approval of Minutes...
a. Local Government Committee...
concerns.output_quote: The JSON uses "type": "list", "list_type": "unordered" for multiple fragmented items (e.g., elements 9, 11) and strips the prefix 'a.' from the content in element 24.

concerns.analysis: A hyperlink to a .gov resource is present in the source PDF (section 6.e, on the text 'open source document'). This link is entirely missing from the output because the paragraph containing the link was not captured.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: An item to work on is an update to the open source document to include...
concerns.output_quote: The output contains no link object or text corresponding to the paragraph under section 6.e.

concerns.analysis: In the source PDF, the meeting minutes are organized into a continuous numbered list from 1 to 9 (1. Welcome, 2. Approval..., through 9. Adjourn), where each number represents a top-level agenda item of equal hierarchy. In the HTML output, the visual organization is significantly altered. Items 1 through 5 are demoted to a bulleted list nested under the 'MINUTES' section, and they are entirely omitted from the navigation Table of Contents. Conversely, items 6, 7, 8, and 9 are rendered as numbere
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered list items 1 through 9 (e.g., '1. Welcome', '2. Approval of Minutes...', up to '9. Adjourn') are top-level headings on pages 1, 2, and 3.
concerns.output_quote: Items 1-5 are rendered as a bulleted list within the body and are missing from the TOC (e.g., '* Welcome', '* Approval of Minutes...'), while items 6-9 (e.g., '6. Status Reports') are rendered as primary headings included in the TOC.
concerns.location: Throughout the document structure, specifically comparing the sections before and after item 6.

action_items: Restore missing paragraph content for Sections 6 and 8.
Fix list structures to ensure agenda items 1-9 are top-level headers or high-level list items.
Re-insert the missing hyperlink in section 6.e.
